document.title: MCMIS Vehicle Inspection Data Set Dictionary

vehicle_inspection_file.description: Main inspection record table containing header-level data for each vehicle inspection.

fields.description: Date & time record was last updated
Unique identifying number assigned to an inspection record
USDOT number assigned by MCMIS to a census record
State/District/Province which uploads the inspection
Number identifying the inspection report
fields.format: SS######## (SS=state code or US for Federal)

fields.description: Date the inspection was conducted
Time the inspection started
Time the inspection ended
Date the certification of correction of defects was registered
FHWA/FMCSA region in which the inspection took place

values.1: CT, ME, MA, NH, NY, NJ, RI, VT, PR, VI
values.3: DE, DC, MD, PA, VA, WV
values.4: AL, GA, FL, MS, NC, SC, TN, KY
values.5: IL, IN, MI, OH, MN, WI
values.8: CO, MT, ND, SD, UT, WY
values.9: AZ, CA, NV, HI, GU, AS

fields.description: Status of the inspection
State assigned code representing the physical location of the inspection
Physical location where the inspection took place
State/District/Province of the county where the inspection took place
FIPS code identifying the county in which the inspection occurred
Level of the inspection
Service Center Region the inspection occurred in
USDOT NUMBER verification method
Type of facility where the inspection was conducted
Name of the shipping company
Unique number on the shipping papers or NONE if not present
Three-character indicator of the cargo tank type (from ASPEN)
Indicates if hazardous material placards are required
SafetyNet version number
SafetyNet search date and time
Whether an alcohol or controlled substance check was performed
Whether there was a drug interdiction search
Number of drug interdiction arrests made
Whether the inspection was conducted in conjunction with a size/weight enforcement action
Whether the inspection was conducted in conjunction with a traffic enforcement action
Whether the inspection was conducted by a local jurisdiction
Indicates use of ASPEN carrier match
Date and time the Status Code was changed
Whether or not the inspection was conducted after an accident
Total gross weight of the combination vehicle in pounds
Total number of violations for the inspection
Total number of out-of-service violations for the inspection
Total number of driver violations for the inspection
Total number of driver out-of-service violations for the inspection
Total number of vehicle violations for the inspection
Total number of vehicle out-of-service violations for the inspection
Total number of hazardous material violations for the inspection
Total number of hazardous material out-of-service violations for the inspection
SafetyNet sequence ID
Reason for upload from SafetyNet
Date and time that transaction code was entered
Inspection upload date and time
Upload census first byte
USDOT number uploaded in the inspection
Upload search indicator
Date and time that census number was searched
Date the inspection was input into SafetyNet
The office that sent the inspection
Date and time the inspection was added to MCMIS
Name of the carrier (individual, partnership, or corporation responsible for transportation)
Street address of the principal place of business of the carrier
Municipality of the principal place of business of the carrier
State/District/Province of the principal place of business of the carrier
ZIP code of the principal place of business of the carrier
Carrier's Mexican Colonia (for Mexican domiciled carriers)
Docket number (MC, MX, or FF number) assigned by FMCSA or the former ICC
Whether the carrier is involved in interstate commerce
Identification number assigned by the state to the carrier

inspections_per_unit.description: Contains vehicle unit details for each inspected unit tied to an inspection record.

fields.description: Date & time record was last updated
Unique identifying number assigned to an inspection record
Unique identifying number assigned to an inspected unit
Code identifying the type of vehicle unit
Sequence number of the vehicle unit (1-6)
Manufacturer of the vehicle unit
Number assigned to the vehicle unit by the carrier
License tag number on the vehicle unit
State/district/province issuing the license tags
Vehicle Identification Number (VIN) of the unit
Whether a CVSA decal was issued
Number on the CVSA decal

vehicle_inspections_and_violations.description: Contains individual violation records associated with each inspection.

fields.description: Date & time record was last updated
Unique identifying number assigned to an inspection record
Unique identifying number assigned to a violation
Sequence number of the violation
Part number of the violation cited (Reference FMCSR, HMR)
Section number of the violation cited (Reference FMCSR, HMR)
Driver, co-driver, or vehicle unit number which had the violation
Unique identifying number assigned to an inspected unit
Category number assigned to the violation (1-99)
Whether the violation resulted in an out-of-service
Defect verification status
Number identifying the citation

special_studies.description: Contains data collected as part of special studies tied to inspections.

fields.description: Date & time record was last updated
Unique identifying number assigned to an inspection record
Unique identifying number assigned to a study
Data collected as part of a special study
Sequence number of the special study (indicates data is in Study field 1, 2, 3, 4, or 5)

inspections_and_citations.description: Contains citation records associated with inspection violations.

fields.description: Date & time record was last updated
Unique identifying number assigned to an inspection record
Sequence number of a violation
Adjusted sequence number of a violation
Citation code
Result of the citation

driver_violations.description: No Log Book, Log Not Current, General Log Violations
Failure to Obey Traffic Control Device
Failure to yield right of way

glossary.BUS: Any motor vehicle designed, constructed, and used for the commercial transportation of 15 or more passengers, including the driver.
glossary.COMMERCIAL_VEHICLE: A motor vehicle, usually a truck or bus, which transports freight or passengers for compensation.
glossary.CVSA: Commercial Vehicle Safety Alliance — an organization of states and provinces in the US, Canada, and Mexico dedicated to improving uniformity of commercial motor vehicle safety enforcement.
glossary.DEFECT_GROUP: The group to which a given violation is attributed: vehicle, driver, or hazardous materials.
glossary.DOUBLE: A commercial motor vehicle consisting of a truck-tractor and two detachable trailers.
glossary.DRIVER_ONLY_INSPECTION: Examines only driver-related aspects of the standard Full Inspection (Level III).
glossary.DRIVER_VIOLATION: A violation discovered during the inspection which pertains to the driver of the commercial vehicle.
glossary.DURATION: The amount of time required to complete a given inspection, calculated from start and finish times.
glossary.FACILITY_TYPE: The type of facility — fixed or roadside — where the inspection was conducted.
glossary.FMCSA: Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration — the administration within USDOT responsible for monitoring and developing safety standards for commercial motor vehicles.
glossary.FMCSR: Federal Motor Carrier Safety Regulations — regulations governing safe operation of commercial vehicles engaged in interstate commerce (Title 49, CFR).
glossary.FIXED_FACILITY: A state commercial vehicle scale facility or other permanent site used to conduct inspections.
glossary.FLEET_SIZE: Total number of power units (truck-tractors and straight trucks) owned or operated by a given motor carrier.
glossary.FOR_HIRE_CARRIER: A commercial motor carrier whose primary business activity is the transportation of property or passengers by motor vehicle for compensation.
glossary.FOR_HIRE_CARRIER_AUTHORIZED: A for-hire carrier subject to economic regulation by the Interstate Commerce Commission.
glossary.FOR_HIRE_CARRIER_EXEMPT: A for-hire carrier not subject to economic regulation by the Interstate Commerce Commission.
glossary.FULL_INSPECTION: The most comprehensive inspection type (Level I), involving extensive vehicle checks including under-vehicle brake measurement and hours-of-service log examination.
glossary.HAZARDOUS_MATERIALS: Materials, substances, or wastes which may be toxic, harmful or fatal if accidentally exposed to humans, animals, or the environment.
glossary.HMR: Hazardous Materials Regulations — federal regulations governing commercial transportation of hazardous materials (Title 49, CFR).
glossary.HAZARDOUS_MATERIALS_VIOLATION: A violation discovered during the inspection which pertains to the transportation of hazardous materials.
glossary.INSPECTION: The systematic examination of a commercial motor vehicle and its driver to determine their overall safety fitness.
glossary.INSPECTION_LEVEL: The inspection methodology employed: Full (I), Walk-Around (II), Driver-Only (III), Special (IV), and Terminal (V).
glossary.INTERSTATE_CARRIER: A carrier who sometimes or always operates in interstate or foreign commerce, including carriers of hazardous materials.
glossary.INTRASTATE_CARRIER: A carrier who operates solely in intrastate commerce and never transports hazardous materials.
glossary.LOCATION: The US State or Territory, Canadian Province, or Mexican State in which a specific inspection was conducted.
glossary.MOBILE_INSPECTION_FACILITY: A non-permanent inspection facility that can be moved from one location to another (roadside facility).
glossary.MOTOR_CARRIER_CENSUS_DATABASE: A database on the USDOT mainframe containing information identifying interstate and intrastate commercial carriers, each assigned a unique USDOT Number.
glossary.MCMIS: Motor Carrier Management Information System — the computerized system operated by FMCSA containing comprehensive safety data on commercial carriers.
glossary.MCSAP: Motor Carrier Safety Assistance Program — a Federal program providing funds to US states and territories for commercial motor vehicle safety activities.
glossary.OOS_VIOLATIONS: Out-of-Service violations — a violation of FMCSR or HMR requiring that a vehicle or driver be taken out of service until corrected.
glossary.PRIVATE_CARRIER: A commercial motor carrier for which private highway transportation activities are incidental to its primary business.
glossary.SAFETYNET: A state-based information system used to store and process commercial carrier safety information in a standard format.
glossary.SINGLE: A commercial motor vehicle consisting of a truck-tractor and a detachable trailer.
glossary.SPECIAL_STUDY: Ad hoc examination of particular items in support of a study or verification of a trend (Level IV).
glossary.STRAIGHT_TRUCK: A commercial motor vehicle in which the power unit and cargo box are non-detachable.
glossary.TERMINAL_INSPECTION: Examination of vehicles at carriers' terminal facilities using Walk-Around technique (Level V).
glossary.TRIPLE: A commercial motor vehicle consisting of a truck-tractor and three detachable trailers.
glossary.TRUCK_TRACTOR: A self-propelled motor vehicle designed and primarily used to draw other vehicles.
glossary.USDOT_NUMBER: An identification number assigned to all interstate commercial carriers regulated by FMCSA for tracking safety records.
glossary.VEHICLE_CONFIGURATION: Arrangement of the individual units (truck-tractors, trailers, etc.) comprising a commercial vehicle.
glossary.VEHICLE_VIOLATION: A violation of the FMCSR or HMR.
glossary.WALK_AROUND_INSPECTION: Follows most procedures of the Full Inspection except those requiring climbing underneath the vehicle (Level II).

reference_data.county_codes_note: FIPS county codes are provided for all US states, territories, and Puerto Rico. Codes are numeric and state-specific. See the full county code listing in the source document (pages 14-84).
